prompt:
Create one polished business visual review image for the prompt family: Product Proof Crop.
Digital product anchor: CompeteIQ.
Product UI and workflow facts to preserve: SaaS app for competitor intelligence reports. Product proof should be the interface and report workflow: paste competitor URLs, add business context, generate a structured 7-section AI competitor report in about 60 seconds. Preserve exact product facts: CompeteIQ name, one free report, no credit card required, secure billing via Stripe, report sections include Executive Summary, Market Gaps, Recommended Actions, Competitor Summaries, Positioning Analysis, Pricing Analysis, Strengths and Weaknesses. Audience: founders, solo operators, consultants, agencies, and small businesses who need practical competitor research quickly. Launch tone: trustworthy, clear, direct, practical, low-hype. Approved copy only: Generate your first report free; One free report, no credit card required; Know what your competitors are actually doing; Paste competitor URLs. Get a 7-section intelligence report in about 60 seconds. Avoid fake customer data, fake logos, fake testimonials, fake ratings, unsupported revenue claims, enterprise complexity, generic AI hype, dark illegible dashboards, and invented partner marks. Use placeholder competitor names only when needed, such as Rival A or Acme Analytics.
The visual must be clearly about this digital product and should not introduce another digital product, app, SaaS workflow, or UI category.
Family intent: A tight product proof ad where the approved UI occupies most of the frame and the surrounding scene stays minimal.
Business goal: prove the digital product visually before adding messaging, claims, or lifestyle context.
Scene: a large, central, inspectable app or browser surface with restrained environment, clean edge detail, and controlled negative space.
Ad format: screen-first proof crop.
Human presence: none.
Rendering mode: premium digital product composite.
Channel fit: paid social, launch post, product page hero, sales deck opener.
Digital product proof: make the product UI, workflow state, and interface feedback the visual evidence.
UI fidelity: use sparse, plausible labels and placeholder data only; do not invent real customer names, private data, or unsupported metrics.
Do not drift into generic abstract SaaS imagery when UI/product proof is requested.
Focus: the exact product UI surface and the interface mechanic that makes the product recognizable.
Decision being supported: whether a direct UI proof crop should lead the campaign.
Reusable prompt pattern: Create a premium screen-first product proof ad for CompeteIQ. Native rule: The product UI itself must be the hero. The screen should occupy roughly 70-85% of the composition and remain large, central, inspectable, and product-specific. Show the approved core UI surface described in the product facts clearly in the approved device policy. Use the supplied brand palette and material cues only as atmosphere. Do not redesign the UI, invent fake data, add claims, metrics, reviews, partner logos, app-store badges, or exterior body copy. Avoid the avoid list and fidelity risks in the product facts. Product facts: SaaS app for competitor intelligence reports. Product proof should be the interface and report workflow: paste competitor URLs, add business context, generate a structured 7-section AI competitor report in about 60 seconds. Preserve exact product facts: CompeteIQ name, one free report, no credit card required, secure billing via Stripe, report sections include Executive Summary, Market Gaps, Recommended Actions, Competitor Summaries, Positioning Analysis, Pricing Analysis, Strengths and Weaknesses. Audience: founders, solo operators, consultants, agencies, and small businesses who need practical competitor research quickly. Launch tone: trustworthy, clear, direct, practical, low-hype. Approved copy only: Generate your first report free; One free report, no credit card required; Know what your competitors are actually doing; Paste competitor URLs. Get a 7-section intelligence report in about 60 seconds. Avoid fake customer data, fake logos, fake testimonials, fake ratings, unsupported revenue claims, enterprise complexity, generic AI hype, dark illegible dashboards, and invented partner marks. Use placeholder competitor names only when needed, such as Rival A or Acme Analytics.
Copy rules: headline: Use exact supplied exterior headline only; otherwise omit.; supportingText: No body copy, metrics, reviews, badges, or benefit bullets unless supplied.; cta: Use exact supplied CTA only; otherwise omit..
Include: large approved UI surface, minimal premium stage, small product-name lockup only if supplied, clean safe space.
Constraints: UI must stay large and inspectable; do not create a generic dashboard; do not add exterior copy unless supplied; do not invent product data; keep text sparse; use short labels, UI placeholder blocks, or visual markers only when needed; keep the product UI, workflow, and product-proof cues recognizable.
Avoid: generic phone/laptop ad, tiny UI, fake analytics, fake product data, unsupported claims, app-store badges, partner logos; fake endorsements; unsupported claims; misspelled large text; watermarks; clutter; other brands.

Use placeholder competitor names only when needed, such as Rival A or Acme Analytics.
